title: Iran Tobacco Market, Post-Privatization Snapshot (2018) — Consumption, Import Dependency, Market Share
source_org: Tobacco Asia (trade publication), citing Iran's Ministry of Health and Ministry of Industries, Mining, and Trade
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION via WebFetch of a single trade-press article.
units: mixed: cigarettes/year, people, percent -- see unit column per row
license_terms: Trade-press article content, used here for factual citation with attribution.
notes: Documents the dramatic reversal of Iran's century-old state tobacco monopoly: the Iranian Tobacco Company (ITC, successor to the historic Dokhaniyat) was privatized in 2012 and by 2018 held only ~15-20% domestic market share, having been overtaken by multinational Japan Tobacco International (JTI, >50% share). Also documents Iran's collapse from tobacco self-sufficiency (13,500 tons/year, 1940, see sibling dataset) to 80% import dependency on tobacco leaf by 2018. This is a companion/bookend dataset to iran-tobacco-monopoly/iranica-tobacco-monopoly-narrative-series-1890-1995/, closing the loop from state monopoly's founding (1890 Regie / 1929 Dokhaniyat) to its effective end.
